author: vasudeva ghoṣa
verses: eka⋅mukhe ki kahiba goracandera līla hāmāguḍi nānā range jaya śacī-bālā lāle mukha jhara jhara dekhite sundara pākā bimba⋅phala jini sundara adhara angada balayā sobhe su⋅bāhu⋅yugale caraṇe magarā khāḍu bāgha⋅nakha gale sonāra sikali pīthe pātera khopanā vāsudeva ghoṣa kahe nichani āpanā
en_translation:
With only one mouth, what can I say of the pastimes of Lord Gorācānd? All glories to the Son of Mother Śacī, who is crawling in various playful moods!

His reddish face is captivating and beautiful to behold. His beautiful lips conquer the beauty of the ripe bimba fruit.

Armlets and bracelets adorn His beautiful arms. He wears anklets on His feet and a tiger-claw pendant on His neck.

A golden chain is upon His back, and His hair is in a silken braid. Vāsudeva Ghoṣa says: "I offer my very self as a sacrifice to Him."
